seed_instruction: Design a birthday party plan for a 5-year-old, specifying games, snacks, and decorations.
org_constraint: The response must contain exactly 10 sentences, with each paragraph containing at least 3 sentences.
new_constraint: The response must contain four paragraphs.
instruction: Design a birthday party plan for a 5-year-old, specifying games, snacks, and decorations. The response must contain exactly 10 sentences, with each paragraph containing at least 3 sentences. The response must contain four paragraphs.
response:
This vibrant birthday party plan is perfect for a fun-filled five-year-old celebration. We will theme the party around a magical "Enchanted Forest" to spark their imagination. Decorations should include green and brown streamers, paper toadstools, and fairy lights draped throughout the party area. Balloons shaped like woodland creatures will add a whimsical touch to the setting.

For engaging activities, a "Pin the Horn on the Unicorn" game is always a huge hit with this age group. Another excellent option is a simple scavenger hunt where they search for hidden "magic berries" (small plastic balls). To keep the energy up, a bubble-blowing station will provide endless entertainment for all the little guests.

The snack menu should be as enchanting as the decor. Instead of typical cake slices, serve "Fairy Wands" (pretzel sticks dipped in colored chocolate) and "Mossy Muffins" (green-tinted cupcakes). Fruit skewers arranged like tiny forest logs will offer a healthy and colorful alternative.

Finally, the party favors should match the theme perfectly. Each child can take home a small bag filled with glittery crowns, nature-themed stickers, and a packet of seeds to plant their own "forest." This thoughtful combination of games, treats, and decor promises a truly memorable birthday adventure.<turn|>
judged_response:
This vibrant birthday party plan is perfect for a fun-filled five-year-old celebration. We will theme the party around a magical "Enchanted Forest" to spark their imagination. Decorations should include green and brown streamers, paper toadstools, and fairy lights draped throughout the party area. Balloons shaped like woodland creatures will add a whimsical touch to the setting.

For engaging activities, a "Pin the Horn on the Unicorn" game is always a huge hit with this age group. Another excellent option is a simple scavenger hunt where they search for hidden "magic berries" (small plastic balls). To keep the energy up, a bubble-blowing station will provide endless entertainment for all the little guests.

The snack menu should be as enchanting as the decor. Instead of typical cake slices, serve "Fairy Wands" (pretzel sticks dipped in colored chocolate) and "Mossy Muffins" (green-tinted cupcakes). Fruit skewers arranged like tiny forest logs will offer a healthy and colorful alternative.

Finally, the party favors should match the theme perfectly. Each child can take home a small bag filled with glittery crowns, nature-themed stickers, and a packet of seeds to plant their own "forest." This thoughtful combination of games, treats, and decor promises a truly memorable birthday adventure.
